user_query:
Read input/tasks.json and input/constraints.yaml. Using a multi-agent orchestration approach, do the following and write all artifacts under output/:

1) Create output/workflow.yaml that defines:
   - routing_rules for the task types in the input (strategy, production, coding_and_scoring, classification). Each rule must list the primary model for that type exactly as specified in the constraint policy and include a short textual trigger description.
   - fallback_chains with three named chains and exact model ordering:
     - reasoning: [claude-opus-4-6, gpt-4o, claude-sonnet-4-6, gemini-2.5-pro]
     - production: [claude-sonnet-4-6, gpt-4o, grok-3]
     - classification: [claude-haiku-4-5, claude-sonnet-4-6]

2) Create output/plan.json that routes every task in input/tasks.json to a single primary model and channel using the deterministic policy in input/constraints.yaml. For each task, include an object with fields: id, assigned_model, channel ("subscription" or "api"), and fallback_chain (one of reasoning, production, classification). The mapping must strictly follow the constraint rules.

3) Create output/qc_traces.jsonl containing exactly two lines: one for task T3 and one for task T4. Each line should be a JSON object with schema:
   {
     "task_id": "<ID>",
     "steps": [
       {"step": 1, "name": "produce",      "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6", "timestamp": "<ISO-8601>", "notes": "<text>"},
       {"step": 2, "name": "review",       "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6", "timestamp": "<ISO-8601>", "notes": "<text>"},
       {"step": 3, "name": "cross_check",  "model": "gpt-4o",            "timestamp": "<ISO-8601>", "notes": "<text>"},
       {"step": 4, "name": "incorporate",  "model": "claude-opus-4-6",   "timestamp": "<ISO-8601>", "notes": "<text>"},
       {"step": 5, "name": "deliver",      "model": "claude-opus-4-6",   "timestamp": "<ISO-8601>", "confidence": <0-100 integer>, "unresolved_concerns": [] }
     ]
   }
The five steps must implement the Produce→Review→Cross-Check→Incorporate→Deliver loop with the exact model assignments shown above and include ISO-8601 timestamps. The final deliver step must include an integer confidence between 0 and 100 and an array field unresolved_concerns (empty array is fine).

Notes:
- Use only the models and chains listed above; do not introduce other models.
- Apply the deterministic channel selection policy from input/constraints.yaml (interactive → subscription; else if batch → api; else if volume > 1000 → api; else → api).
- Ensure all files are valid YAML/JSON/JSONL.
- Do not write any files outside output/.
